code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # simple verb mapping for readability
    verb_map = {
        "cityServed": "serves",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "has an elevation of",
        "location": "is located at",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of",
        "runwayName": "has runway named",
        "country": "is in",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway length of",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "has a first runway surface of",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "has a third runway length of",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code",
        "locationIdentifier": "has location identifier",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "has elevation of",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code",
        "nativeName": "has native name",
        "leaderParty": "belongs to party",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leader": "is led by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway length of",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "has a fourth runway surface of",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "has fifth runway number",
        "largestCity": "has largest city",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "has a fourth runway length of",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has first runway number",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "has elevation of",
        "administrativeArrondissement": "is in administrative arrondissement",
        "mayor": "has mayor",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "has second runway surface of",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "has third runway surface of",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has runway surface of",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language",
        "city": "is city of",
        "jurisdiction": "has jurisdiction over",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "operates helicopter",
        "transportAircraft": "operates transport aircraft",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "headquarter": "has headquarter at",
        "class": "belongs to class",
        "division": "belongs to division",
        "order": "belongs to order",
        "regionServed": "serves region",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "hubAirport": "has hub airport",
        "aircraftFighter": "operates fighter aircraft",
        "attackAircraft": "operates attack aircraft",
        "battle": "participated in battle",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "has fifth runway surface of",
        "countySeat": "has county seat",
        "chief": "has chief",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by",
        "postalCode": "has postal code",
        "areaCode": "has area code",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in",
        "ceremonialCounty": "is in ceremonial county",
    }
    # Group predicates by subject to build a single complex sentence per subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    import re
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, "has")
        obj = t.object
        # Strip surrounding quotes if present
        if isinstance(obj, str):
            obj = obj.strip('\"\'')
        # Add appropriate units for known predicates
        if re.search(r"Feet$", t.predicate):
            # assume numeric value, add 'feet'
            obj = f"{obj} feet"
        elif re.search(r"Metre$", t.predicate) or re.search(r"Metres$", t.predicate):
            obj = f"{obj} metres"
        elif t.predicate in {"elevationAboveTheSeaLevel", "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres"}:
            obj = f"{obj} metres above sea level"
        elif t.predicate == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet":
            obj = f"{obj} feet above sea level"
        elif t.predicate == "runwayLength":
            obj = f"{obj} metres"
        elif t.predicate == "runwaySurfaceType":
            obj = f"{obj.lower()}"
        # Build phrase
        subject_map[t.subject].append(f"{verb} {obj}")

    parts = []
    for subj, actions in subject_map.items():
        if len(actions) == 1:
            parts.append(f"{subj} {actions[0]}")
        else:
            # Join multiple actions with commas and an 'and' before the last one
            if len(actions) == 2:
                actions_str = " and ".join(actions)
            else:
                actions_str = ", ".join(actions[:-1]) + ", and " + actions[-1]
            parts.append(f"{subj} {actions_str}")

    # Combine different subjects with semicolons and finish with a period
    return "; ".join(parts) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
